title: Graduate Alumni Community
description: Jointly established by New York University and East China Normal University, NYU Shanghai is the third degree-granting campus in NYU’s global network, joining NYU Abu Dhabi and NYU's campus in New York City.
text:
Graduate Alumni Community
Over the past decade, NYU Shanghai has welcomed advanced degree students from every province in China and 17 countries worldwide, ranging in age from 21 to 63. More than 400 have graduated from PhD and master’s programs so far. Following graduation, many choose to stay in Shanghai, while some choose to move to other cities in China or abroad like Australia, Los Angeles, New York City, Poland, Saudi Arabia, and Singapore for employment or further study. They join a worldwide NYU alumni community of 600,000 strong, having earned an unparalleled NYU education while enjoying the unique advantages of study at NYU Shanghai.
Notable Alumni Events
NYU Shanghai & Hopkins-Nanjing Center Alumni & Graduate Students Gathering
On March 22, 2025, alumni and current graduate students from NYU Shanghai, the Hopkins-Nanjing Center (HNC)/Johns Hopkins School of Advanced International Studies (SAIS) gathered at the NYU Shanghai New Bund Campus for an enriching intellectual exchange and networking experience. Eric Mao, Dean of Graduate and Advanced Education at NYU Shanghai, opened the event by highlighting the growing exchange between NYU Shanghai and HNC, expressing excitement about the opportunities ahead.
The event featured a roundtable discussion on the future of human-AI interaction, with faculty from both institutions, including Qingwen Xu, Tingjun Zheng, Xiaorong Li, Adam Webb, and Thomas Simon. The conversation explored the intersection of AI with social work, business, political science, philosophy, and beyond.
Alumni and graduate students engaged in lively discussions, fostering connections across diverse academic and professional backgrounds. The event concluded with a campus tour, offering attendees a glimpse of the vibrant environment at NYU Shanghai. The gathering provided a valuable space for exchanging ideas and perspectives, further deepening the bond between NYU Shanghai, HNC, and SAIS alumni and graduate students.
NYU Shanghai & Duke Kunshan University Alumni and Graduate Students Gathering
On April 14th, 2024, alumni and current students from 10 graduate programs of both NYU Shanghai and Duke Kunshan University gathered at NYU Shanghai New Bund Campus. In his opening remarks, Eric Mao, Dean of Graduate and Advanced Education at NYU Shanghai, recognized the resilience and potential of the two institutions, appreciated the spirit of international cooperation embodied in NYU Shanghai and Duke Kunshan, both Sino-US universities, and encouraged attendees to forge connections with peers of different stripes. Dean Mao also extended a special welcome to Dr. Xin Li, Associate Vice-Chancellor for Graduate Studies and Research at Duke Kunshan University, with both expressing enthusiasm for future collaborations.
Alumni and students received university swags upon check-in and quickly bonded over team trivia covering interesting facts about NYU, NYU Shanghai, Duke University, and DKU. Alumni and students from the Class of 2018 to 2025 caught up with old friends while meeting new ones, engaged in dynamic conversations across disciplines and industries, and shared experiences with each other. The event concluded with a tour of the NYU Shanghai New Bund Campus.
Graduate Alumni 10th Anniversary Homecoming Gala
Nearly 100 NYU Shanghai graduate alumni joined in the reunion celebrations at the Graduate Alumni 10th Anniversary Homecoming Gala organized by the Office of Graduate and Advanced Education.
“What a thrill to welcome back so many alumni from virtually every past graduating class of PhD, master’s, and go local students from 2017 through 2022,” said Dean of Graduate and Advanced Education Eric Mao. “On this celebratory occasion, we also recognize all of the partners, both within and outside our university, in Shanghai and New York, who made these successes possible. During today’s Homecoming Gala, we look back and look forward toward the goal of an ever more vibrant and united community around graduate education at NYU Shanghai.”
Faculty who shared remarks included Affiliated Assistant Professor of Social Work Minchao Jin, Assistant Professor of Practice in Business Analytics Ming Liao, Capstone Director of the MS in Quantitative Finance (MSQF) Han Shen Lin, and Professor June Bu. Director of TESOL Program Kentei Takaya and Director of the Master of Arts Program in Interactive Media Arts (IMA) Craig Protzel sent greetings by way of pre-recorded video messages. The graduate alumni then concluded their evening with a tour of the new campus.
NYU peer schools also sent their greetings.“Congratulations on the opening of the New Bund campus, and on a successful 10 years despite so many challenges—here's to a great future ahead!” said Dean of NYU Stern School of Business Raghu Sundaram in his message, one of the many congratulatory messages the Office received. “Stern is proud to be a part of this journey, and we look forward to our continued collaboration in the years to come.”
Selected Alumni Features
Huang Fangling, IMA '22: Stay Curious and Keep Exploring
Yanqiu Wu, PhD '22: When the Going Gets Tough, the Tough Get Going
Forrest Feng, MA TESOL '22: A Lawyer who Loves Teaching English
Gallery
